portfolio.name: Portfolio
portfolio.description: A personal portfolio with intro, projects, and contact.

props.text:
Hi, I'm Alex.
I build web things.
Full-stack developer focused on product UI, design systems, and the occasional CLI tool.

I work with TypeScript, React, and a little bit of Python. I like systems that feel calm — small surfaces, clear types, and UI that doesn't fight you. Outside of work I sketch, lift heavy things, and read more than I should.

props.items:
Sketchpad — a no-account whiteboard for quick diagrams
Pulse — a tiny self-hosted uptime monitor
Lumen — a documentation theme for fast-moving teams
Forge — a CLI for scaffolding typed APIs

props.text: Drop a few lines about what you're working on. I read everything.

blog.name: Blog post
blog.description: Long-form writing layout with title, body, and tags.

props.metaDescription: A short description of this post.

props.text: Calm software is software that asks for your attention only when it has something to say. The rest of the time it stays out of the way.

One: make defaults that work for most people. Two: surface a single primary action per view. Three: respect the user's time — every animation, every modal, every confirmation is a place to lose trust.
“A notification is a debt. Spend them only on things worth interrupting for.”
None of this is novel. The interesting part is doing it consistently, in the small, when the deadline is Friday and the dropdown already works.

props.text: Complete redesign of a B2B notification platform. Reduced cognitive load by 40% and improved trial-to-paid conversion.
Marketing site and onboarding flow for a personal finance app targeting young professionals. Tailwind CSS + Alpine.js.
Bold editorial site for a Paris-based branding studio. Scroll-driven animations and custom cursor to match their premium positioning.
A bit about who I am

I'm Eliott, a freelance designer and frontend developer based in Paris with 5 years of experience shipping digital products for startups, agencies, and scale-ups across Europe. I thrive at the intersection of great design and clean code.
I believe great interfaces are invisible — they get out of the user's way. My work is fast, accessible and built to last. When I'm not coding, you'll find me hiking or hunting for a good espresso.
